channel.title: Frontion News
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: USS Gerald R. Ford Returns After Record 11-Month Deployment Supporting Iran War and Venezuela Operation
item.description: ⚓🇺🇸 The worlds largest aircraft carrier returned to Virginia after 326 days at sea, the longest deployment since Vietnam War, having supported combat operations against Iran and the capture of Venezuelas Maduro. The crew received the Presidential Unit Citation for outstanding performance in combat.

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency
item.description: 🦠🌍 The World Health Organization declared a public health emergency of international concern after over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from the Bundibugyo virus variant, which has no approved treatments or vaccines. Cases have spread from Congo to Ugandas capital Kampala.

item.title: Trump Hesitates on Taiwan Arms Sales After Xi Warning During Beijing Summit
item.description: 🇨🇳🇺🇸🇹🇼 Following his three-day China visit, Trump said he has not decided whether to proceed with a $25 billion Taiwan arms package after hearing Xi Jinping warn that Taiwanese independence would mean confrontation. The summit produced a new US-China framework of strategic stability focused on managed competition.

item.title: Russia Launches Largest Drone Assault Since War Began, Flattens Kyiv Apartment Building
item.description: 🇺🇦⚔️🇷🇺 Russia fired over 1,560 drones and missiles across Ukraine in three days, killing at least 9 in Kyiv when a nine-story apartment building collapsed. The massive strikes came during Trumps Beijing summit and followed a brief ceasefire, with Ukraine saying air defenses intercepted over 93 percent of incoming targets.

item.title: Pentagon Cancels Europe Troop Deployment Amid Frustrations With NATO Over Iran War
item.description: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 The Pentagon withdrew thousands of soldiers from a planned deployment to Poland as Trump expressed anger over European allies refusal to support the war with Iran. The move signals deepening US frustration with NATO burden-sharing as the Iran conflict strains alliance relations.
